political.s: Jack in the Box made minimal federal PAC contributions ($9,583 in 2024) and reported zero lobbying spending in 2024. A previous state-level PAC terminated in 2016 focused on California legislative contributions.

charity.s: Jack in the Box Foundation, established in 1998, supports youth-focused causes including food insecurity and mentorship. The company has raised over $6 million for No Kid Hungry since 2015 and contributed over $7 million to Big Brothers Big Sisters since 1998.
charity.focus: childhood hunger, youth mentorship, March of Dimes

environment.s: Jack in the Box published its first sustainability report in 2023, committing to 20% reduction in virgin plastic by 2030 and 100% PFA-free packaging. However, it received an F rating from As You Sow in 2020 for packaging sustainability and Sustainalytics rates it as severely misaligned with 1.5°C warming targets.

labor.s: Jack in the Box has faced multiple wage and labor violations including misclassification of exempt managers in California (2017) and a 2026 Ninth Circuit ruling requiring full meal period pay. The company ranks in the bottom 5% for compensation and benefits on Comparably.

dei.s: Jack in the Box has a Glassdoor DEI rating of 3.7/5 (equal to industry average) and ranks in the bottom 20% for diversity on Comparably with a 55/100 score. Former CEO signed CEO Action for Diversity pledge.

animals.s: Jack in the Box serves meat and dairy products and is listed on Cruelty Free Investing for exploiting animals. The company sources 84% cage-free eggs and committed to 100% gestation-crate-free pork by 2026, meeting interim 25% target in 2024.

guns.s: Not applicable - Jack in the Box is a fast food restaurant chain that does not sell firearms.

privacy.s: Jack in the Box does not store credit card information, using third-party processors and PCI DSS compliance. The company has not experienced a security breach in the last three years but collects extensive location and preference data for marketing purposes.

execPay.s: CEO Lance Tucker earned $5.27M in 2024 (88% bonuses), while average employee compensation is $121K annually. Interim CEO Mark King received 186,901 RSU award in May 2026. CEO-to-median-employee pay ratio significantly exceeds industry norms.
execPay.ratio: 43:1 (estimated based on $5.27M CEO vs $121K median employee)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
